name: financial_services_classroom_monitoring_millhaven_lib_eq
premise: A Financial Services incident command desk is in a fast-moving incident involving financial services classroom monitoring millhaven lib eq. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is before the next automated batch runs. Every delay increases customer access, fraud loss, and regulatory exposure. Stakeholders are already framing this as liberty vs equality under pressure. Every action is being logged and will be reconstructed in an after-action review. Constraints: Ensure a usable human override/appeals path. Preserve evidence under audit and litigation-hold.

agents.name: Marco Vasquez
agents.identity: I am Marco Vasquez, accountable for a defensible call in Financial Services when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays equality for liberty.

agents.name: Omar Santos
agents.identity: I am Omar Santos, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Financial Services.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so equality isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of liberty.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect equality.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to equality to secure liberty under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is before the next automated batch runs. Evidence is partial. Marco Vasquez must decide under liberty vs equality. What does Marco Vasquez do?
